items.headline: Major power upgrade permitted for Porter Drive research facility
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development operations. The work includes installing new transformers and extending power lines to a substation on Hanover Drive, indicating substantial new tenant activity at this commercial site.

items.headline: Historic home conversion: Four townhomes added behind preserved house on Crescent Avenue
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing plans to develop four three-story townhomes behind a 960-square-foot historic single-family home at 434 Crescent Avenue while preserving the original structure. This project balances housing density with historic resource protection in a medium-density residential zone.

items.headline: City replacing Terminal Elevator, contracting encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The City Council approved actions related to replacing an elevator at the Terminal and issued a request for proposals for encampment cleanup services. These decisions will affect downtown infrastructure and the city's approach to managing homeless encampments.

items.headline: San José expands digital advertising on city property with Clear Channel
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC to allow additional large format digital signage on city-owned property. This could increase advertising revenue but may also change the visual character of public spaces.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase at 10480 Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to purchase property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with price and payment terms currently under discussion. The outcome could affect future development or use of this city land.

items.headline: Milpitas Adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide recreational development in this key area of Milpitas. This plan will shape how residents can access green space and outdoor amenities as the district continues to develop.

items.headline: City Council Updates Meeting Procedures and Protocols Handbook
items.summary: The Council approved changes to how it conducts meetings and operates, reflected in updates to the City Council Procedures and Protocols Handbook. These procedural changes may affect how residents can participate in future council meetings.

items.headline: El Paseo De Saratoga permits 772-unit residential complex
items.summary: Two building permits totaling 772 apartments and $125.7 million in construction have been issued for the El Paseo De Saratoga development. This represents a significant housing addition to San José's residential supply.
